details.body:
Hi breaking change review board, we did TypeSpec conversion for Operations resource in 2025-04-01. Now service team is introducing a lower version 2025-03-01, therefore the pipeline Swagger BreackingChange (Cross-Version) is comparing with version 2024-11-01 which is still manual swagger.

There are several places in the original swagger that marked a model as readonly like https://github.com/Azure/azure-rest-api-specs/blob/4634ec1ddab8ec861703028bd9eaaba07ee7729d/specification/kubernetesconfi

events.description: `nextLink` should be omitted when there is no next page. Setting it to `null` won't validate against

details.body: `nextLink` should be omitted when there is no next page. Setting it to `null` won't validate against the swagger schema (string) and conflicts with Azure paging guidance.

events.description: `ExtensionProperties.configurationProtectedSettings` represents sensitive values but is missing `x-m

details.body: `ExtensionProperties.configurationProtectedSettings` represents sensitive values but is missing `x-ms-secret: true` (and is currently modeled as readable via GET/PUT/PATCH responses). This can lead to SDKs treating secrets as normal fields. Mark this property as secret (and, if the service does not return it, ensure it is not included in read responses via mutability/visibility and keep it out of examples).

events.description: The example includes `Operation-Location` and `x-ms-async-operation-timeout` response headers for th

details.body: The example includes `Operation-Location` and `x-ms-async-operation-timeout` response headers for the LRO, but these headers are not defined in the corresponding swagger operation responses (which only define `Azure-AsyncOperation`/`Retry-After`). Update the swagger to declare these headers if they are actually returned, or remove them from the example so it matches the spec.

The generated swagger operations only declare `Azure-AsyncOperation`/`Retry-After`, so these headers will not validate against the spec unless they are added there. Align the example with the swagger (or update the swagger headers and regenerate).

details.body: This listed item has an `id` whose last segment is `BackupVault01`, but the `name` field is `App1Monitor`. For ARM resources, `name` should match the resource name segment of the `id` (and the `{extensionName}` used to address the resource). Update the example so `name` and `id` are consistent.

events.description: The `LroLocationHeader` suppression states that 202 operations return `Azure-Async-Operation` and im

details.body: The `LroLocationHeader` suppression states that 202 operations return `Azure-Async-Operation` and implies `Location`/`Operation-Location` is not present, but the new 202/201 examples include an `Operation-Location` header. Align the swagger header definitions, suppression rationale, and examples (either add the header to the spec or remove it from examples).

details.body:
## Pull request overview

Adds a new stable ARM API version (`2025-03-01`) for **Microsoft.KubernetesConfiguration** Extensions (and corresponding Operations surface), including new swagger outputs and example payloads emitted from TypeSpec.

**Changes:**
- Introduces `stable/2025-03-01` swagger for Extensions and Operations, plus associated examples.
- Updates TypeSpec version enums and models to add new fields/values for `2025-03-01` (e.g., `autoUpgradeMode`, management/additional details, new

details.body: The operation description is missing a trailing period (and should follow the repo convention of full-sentence operation descriptions). Update it to end with a period for consistency.

Align the example with the swagger (or update the swagger headers and regenerate).

details.body: Example/schema mismatch: this example's 202 response includes `Operation-Location` and `x-ms-async-operation-timeout`, but the `PATCH` 202 response headers in `stable/2025-03-01/extensions.json` only define `Azure-AsyncOperation` and `Retry-After`. Please update the swagger to include these headers (if returned) or remove them from the example.

events.description: Documentation accuracy: the suppression reason mentions the `Azure-Async-Operation` header, but the

details.body: Documentation accuracy: the suppression reason mentions the `Azure-Async-Operation` header, but the standard ARM LRO header name (and the one used in the swagger) is `Azure-AsyncOperation` (no extra hyphen). Please correct the header name in this text to avoid confusion.

events.description: Security: `configurationProtectedSettings` is described as sensitive but is not marked with `x-ms-se

details.body: Security: `configurationProtectedSettings` is described as sensitive but is not marked with `x-ms-secret: true` and is part of `ExtensionProperties` (used in GET/PUT/PATCH responses). Per ARM guidelines (no secrets in responses; secrets must be annotated), either model this as write-only (separate request model / mutability create+update only) and ensure it is not returned, and add `x-ms-secret: true` so SDKs treat it as secret.

events.description: API design: `AutoUpgradeMode` has `x-ms-enum` value names `none/patch/compatible` in lowercase. Azur

details.body: API design: `AutoUpgradeMode` has `x-ms-enum` value names `none/patch/compatible` in lowercase. Azure guidelines recommend PascalCase enum member names for better SDK ergonomics (values can remain lowercase). Consider changing the `x-ms-enum.values[*].name` to `None`, `Patch`, `Compatible` while keeping the wire values unchanged.

Please either add these headers to the swagger response definition or remove them from the example so it validates.

events.description: Best practice: `ExtensionStatus.time` is documented as ISO8601 time but is missing a `format: date-t

details.body: Best practice: `ExtensionStatus.time` is documented as ISO8601 time but is missing a `format: date-time`. Consider adding `format: date-time` (RFC3339) so SDKs validate and model it correctly.

## Pull request overview

Updates the generated metadata for `azure-resourcemanager-kubernetesconfiguration-extensions` to align with the latest TypeSpec/REST API spec commit used for generation, and refreshes the package changelog for the stable 1.0.0 release.

**Changes:**
- Updated `tsp-location.yaml` to point at the new `azure-rest-api-specs` commit SHA used for generation.
- Updated `CHANGELOG.md` release date for `1.0.0`.
- Re-ordered a few changelog bullet lines (no functional impact), bu

events.description: The changelog entry for `AksIdentityType.values()` drops generics (`java.util.Collection values()`),

details.body:
The changelog entry for `AksIdentityType.values()` drops generics (`java.util.Collection values()`), but the generated API is `java.util.Collection<AksIdentityType> values()`. Please update the entry to include the generic type (and ideally the fully-qualified model type) to avoid documenting an incorrect/raw return type.
```suggestion
* `models.AksIdentityType[] values()` -> `java.util.Collection<com.azure.resourcemanager.kubernetesconfiguration.extensions.models.AksIdentityType> values()`
```

The previous implementation guarded against `inner` being null; the new version unconditionally dere

details.body: The previous implementation guarded against `inner` being null; the new version unconditionally dereferences it. If `serviceClient().getWithResponse(...)` can ever return null (e.g., in mocked/tested scenarios), this will throw a NullPointerException. If null is not expected, it would be better to enforce that explicitly (e.g., fail fast with a clear exception via `Objects.requireNonNull(inner, ...)`) or restore the null-handling logic to preserve prior behavior.

events.description: The sample passes `null` for the `forceDelete` Boolean argument. For samples, using an explicit valu

details.body:
The sample passes `null` for the `forceDelete` Boolean argument. For samples, using an explicit value (e.g., `false`) is typically clearer for users and avoids implying that `null` is the recommended/default usage.
```suggestion
            .delete("rg1", "Microsoft.Kubernetes", "connectedClusters", "clusterName1", "ClusterMonitor", false,
```

details.body:
This import block isn’t gofmt-compliant: standard-library imports are mixed with module imports and aren’t separated into groups. Running gofmt should reorder/group these imports so stdlib comes first with a blank line before third-party imports.
```suggestion
	"log"

	"github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-go/sdk/azidentity"
	"github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-go/sdk/resourcemanager/kubernetesconfiguration/armextensions"
```

	"time"

`warp.config.yml` no longer specifies `moduleType: commonjs` for the `commonjs` target. In this repo

details.body:
`warp.config.yml` no longer specifies `moduleType: commonjs` for the `commonjs` target. In this repo, other `warp.config.yml` files consistently include `moduleType: commonjs` for the CommonJS target (e.g., `sdk/kubernetesconfiguration/arm-kubernetesconfiguration-fluxconfigurations/warp.config.yml:24-28`). Without it, the warp build may generate incorrect package exports/conditions for the CJS output.

Consider restoring `moduleType: commonjs` under the `commonjs` target to match the repo’s warp

Please restore the removed beta history below the new `1.0.0` entry so the package retains a complete release record.

events.description: `pnpm-lock.yaml` appears to have been regenerated in a way that prunes platform-specific optional de

details.body:
`pnpm-lock.yaml` appears to have been regenerated in a way that prunes platform-specific optional dependencies (e.g., `esbuild@0.27.7` only lists a subset of `@esbuild/*` binaries). This will cause non-deterministic installs and lockfile churn (or even install/build failures) on platforms/architectures that no longer have entries recorded in the lockfile.

Please regenerate the lockfile using the repo’s standard pnpm settings so that all platform-specific optional dependencies are preserved (not

events.description: Similar to the `esbuild` section, the `rollup@4.60.1` entry’s `optionalDependencies` list appears pr

details.body:
Similar to the `esbuild` section, the `rollup@4.60.1` entry’s `optionalDependencies` list appears pruned to a limited set of OS/CPU binaries. This can break deterministic installs and will require contributors on other platforms to rewrite the lockfile during `pnpm install`.

Regenerate `pnpm-lock.yaml` so the lock records all platform-specific optional binary packages for Rollup as well.

events.description: The `turbo@2.9.3` lockfile entry’s `optionalDependencies` list is missing several platform packages

details.body:
The `turbo@2.9.3` lockfile entry’s `optionalDependencies` list is missing several platform packages (for example `@turbo/darwin-arm64`, `@turbo/linux-arm64`, `@turbo/windows-arm64` are no longer present anywhere in the lockfile). This is likely to break `pnpm install` on those platforms or at minimum force lockfile modifications.

Please regenerate `pnpm-lock.yaml` without pruning platform-specific optional dependencies so Turbo’s binaries are available for all supported OS/CPU combinations.

1 tool issue found in `@azure/arm-kubernetesconfiguration-extensions` 1.0.0 (stable release from beta). The CHANGELOG.md is missing prior beta release history (`1.0.0-beta.2` and `1.0.0-beta.1`) — the generation tool wiped it out when creating the stable release entry. Version consistency across `package.json`, `extensionsContext.ts`, and `CHANGELOG.md` is correct. No API design issues found in the public surface.
